content: agent2 here: implementing Global Filter Registry (register_filter/unregister_filter) in outlines/prompts.py. Will touch the Environment/filters setup in prompts.py. Let me know if you're also touching that file.

I already implemented and it's now in the working tree (outlines/prompts.py): module-level _FILTERS: Dict[str, Callable] = {} plus register_filter(name, func) and unregister_filter(name) functions near the top of the file (after imports, before the Prompt dataclass). In _template_from_str and _template_from_file, I added 'env.filters.update(_FILTERS)' right after creating the jinja2.Environment and BEFORE setting the builtin filters (name/description/source/signature/schema/args), so builtin/local filters take precedence over global ones. If you refactor into a create_jinja_env(filters=None) helper, please keep that ordering: env.filters.update(_FILTERS) first, then update with builtin filters, then update with any locally-passed 'filters' dict (so local > builtin > global, or however you want to layer it, just keep global lowest precedence). Feel free to move my two lines into your helper. I'm about to finalize my diff to patch.txt - let me know if you're editing prompts.py right now so I don't stomp on you.
